intro: Pilocereus sublanatus, also known as the "prickly pear cactus," is a species of cactus native to South America, characterized by its branching stems and sharp spines. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it a potential treatment for various skin conditions and infections. The bioactive constituents of Pilocereus sublanatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include topical creams and ointments for wound care, as well as oral supplements in the form of capsules or extracts, which are often used to support skin health and immune function.
